LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT CARLENE LAUGHLIN'S MOTION FOR DETERMINATION OF GOOD FAITH SETTLEMENT IS DENIED AS PREMATURE. A GOOD FAITH SETTLEMENT MOTION CAN BE GRANTED ONLY IF THERE IS NO DISPUTE THAT THERE IS A VALID AND ENFORCEABLE SETTLEMENT. PLAINTIFF TIBOR SZABO APPARENTLY DISPUTES THAT THERE IS A VALID AND ENFORCEABLE SETTLEMENT, AT LEAST IF IT IS CONSTRUED THAT THE SETTLEMENT PAYMENT IS SUBJECT TO A LIEN. MOREOVER, THE COURT HAS DENIED MS. LAUGHLIN'S MOTION TO ENFORCE THE SETTLEMENT AGREEMENT. COURT TO PREPARE FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: CAROL KAREN, CSR #8189

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Sep-27-2017 Off Calendar

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Aug-22-2017 Continued To Oct-11-17 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Ntc Of Mtn To Remove Tim Rumbergers Unauthorized Lien
Off Calendar Aug-07-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Tibor Szabo (415-684-5211) appears as self-represented plaintiff/moving party

Attorney, Michael J. Wess of the law offices of Bennett Samuelson Reynolds et al (510-444-7688) specially appears as counsel for Carlene Laughlin, defendant/non-opposing party

No other appearance is made in this matter

The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

PLAINTIFF TIBOR SZABO's Motion To Remove Tim Rumberger's Unauthorized Lien is granted. The notice of lien filed May 17, 2017 in this case is invalid because it purports to be on behalf of lien claimant and judgment creditor Henry Laughlin, yet Mr. Laughlin is deceased and there is no indication that any representative of his estate authorized the filing of the notice of lien.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: J. Rios Merida; Reporter: Gordon F. Aiavao, CSR No. 11216, email: gaiavao@sftc.org. (D302)

Defendant Carlene Laughlin'S Motion For Determination Of Good Faith Settlement
Off Calendar Jul-06-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - TIBOR SZABO, PLAINTIFF APPEARING IN PRO PER;
ATTY. TIMOTHY P. RUMBERGER, TEL. NO. 1-510-841-5500 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT HENRY LAUGLIN;
ATTY. JULIE L. BATZ OF THE BENNETT, SAMUELSEN REYNODS & GELINI, TEL. NO. 1-510-444-7688 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT CARLENE LAUGHLIN.

RULING - DEFENDANT CARLENE LAUGHLIN'S Motion For Determination Of Good Faith Settlement is
DENIED as premature. A good faith settlement motion can be granted only if there is no dispute that there is a valid and enforceable settlement. Plaintiff Tibor Szabo apparently disputes that there is a valid and enforceable settlement, at least if it is construed that the settlement payment is subject to a lien. Moreover, the court has denied Ms. Laughlin's motion to enforce the settlement agreement. Court to prepare form of order. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR #8189 =302/hk

Defendant Carlene Laughlin'S Motion To Enforce Signed Settlement Agreement
Off Calendar Jul-06-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - TIBOR SZABO, PLAINTIFF APPEARING IN PRO PER;
ATTY. TIMOTHY P. RUMBERGER, TEL. NO. 1-510-841-5500 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT HENRY LAUGLIN;
ATTY. JULIE L. BATZ OF THE BENNETT, SAMUELSEN REYNODS & GELINI, TEL. NO. 1-510-444-7688 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT CARLENE LAUGHLIN.

RULING - A R G U E D; DEFENDANT CARLENE LAUGHLIN'S Motion To Enforce Signed Settlement Agreement is DENIED. Ms. Laughlin invokes the summary procedure of CCP 664.6. Per that section, Ms. Laughlin must show that the "parties" signed a written settlement agreement. (Weddington Productions, Inc. v. Flick (1998) 60 Cal.App.4th 793, 810.) The settlement agreement submitted by Ms. Laughlin, however, is not signed by her and thus the CCP 646.6 procedure is not applicable to that agreement. Signatures from both parties are necessary per CCP 664.6 case law. (Harris v. Rudin, Richman & Appel (1999) 74 Cal.App.4th 299, 304-306.)
Court to prepare form of order. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR #8189 =302/hk

Claimant Henry Laughlin'S Motion For An Order Of Assignment To Enforce Lien, Directing Plaintiffs Rights To Good Faith Settlement Money Be Applied To Satisfy Judgment Creditors Lien
Off Calendar Jul-06-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - TIBOR SZABO, PLAINTIFF APPEARING IN PRO PER;
ATTY. TIMOTHY P. RUMBERGER, TEL. NO. 1-510-841-5500 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT HENRY LAUGLIN;
ATTY. JULIE L. BATZ OF THE BENNETT, SAMUELSEN REYNODS & GELINI, TEL. NO. 1-510-444-7688 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT CARLENE LAUGHLIN.

RULING - JUDGMENT CREDITOR HENRY LAUGHLIN'S Motion For An Order Of Assignment To Enforce Lien, Directing Plaintiffs Rights To Good Faith Settlement Money Be Applied To Satisfy Judgment Creditors Lien is DENIED as premature. Assuming without deciding that such a motion may be made in the name of Mr. Laughlin and not in the name of his estate or by his personal representative, the motion can be granted only if there is no dispute that there is a valid and enforceable settlement between defendant Carlene Laughlin and plaintiff Tibor Szabo. However, Mr. Szabo apparently disputes that there is a valid and enforceable settlement, at least if it is construed that the settlement payment is subject to a lien. Moreover, the court has denied Ms. Laughlin's motion to enforce the settlement agreement. Court to prepare form of order. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR #8189 =302/hk

Defendant Timothy Rumberger'S Special Anti-Slapp Motion To Strike
Off Calendar Jul-06-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - TIBOR SZABO, PLAINTIFF APPEARING IN PRO PER;
ATTY. TIMOTHY P. RUMBERGER, TEL. NO. 1-510-841-5500 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT HENRY LAUGLIN;
ATTY. JULIE L. BATZ OF THE BENNETT, SAMUELSEN REYNODS & GELINI, TEL. NO. 1-510-444-7688 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT CARLENE LAUGHLIN.

RULING - A R G U E D; The court adopted its previous tentative ruling in this matter. DEFENDANT TIMOTHY RUMBERGER'S Special Anti-Slapp Motion To Strike, as to all eight causes of action in the complaint filed by plaintiff Tibor Szabo is GRANTED in its entirety. All eight causes of action arise from Mr. Szabo's allegations that Mr. Rumberger posted false statements about Mr. Szabo on Yelp and other websites and Mr. Rumberger misused the judicial process. (Complaint 3:14-16; 4:2-13; 5:5-12; 5:20-25; 6:17-21; 7:13-16; 9:6-7; 9:20-23.) Mr. Rumberger satisfies his first prong burden of the anti-SLAPP analysis as to all eight causes of action. The posting of consumer information on Yelp implicates protected activity per the anti-SLAPP statute. (Wong v. Tai Jing (2010) 189 Cal.App.4th 1354, 1366-1367.) The allegations that Mr. Rumberger posted information about Mr. Szabo's fraudulent activities and penchant to engage in sexual misconduct raise issues of public interest and the postings were made in public fora. The alleged litigation misconduct also implicates the anti-SLAPP statute and claims for malicious prosecution and abuse of process fall within the scope of protected activity by the anti-SLAPP statute. (Jarrow Formulas, Inc. v. LaMarche (2003) 31 Cal.4th 728, 734-735.) Because Mr. Rumberger satisfied his first prong burden, the burden shifts to Mr. Szabo to present evidence showing at least minimal merit of his claims. Mr. Szabo fails to meet that burden. Mr. Szabo does not present admissible evidence to support any of his claims. Order and Judgment of dismissal as to Defendant Timothy Rumberger is signed. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR #8189

Notice And Motion Of Defendants Special Anti-Slapp Motion To Strike
Off Calendar Jun-29-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES - TIBOR SZABO, PLAINTIFF APPEARING IN PRO PER;
ATTY. JULIE L. BATZ OF THE BENNETT SAMUELSEN REYNOLDS & GELINI LAW OFFICE, TEL. NO. 1-510-444-7688 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT;
TIMOTHY P. RUMBERGER, TEL. NO. 1-510-841-5500, DEFENDANT APPEARING IN PRO PER.

RULING - THE MATTER WAS NOT ARGUED. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT TIMOTHY RUMBERGER'S Special Anti-Slapp Motion To Strike, as to all eight causes of action in the complaint filed by plaintiff Tibor Szabo is GRANTED in its entirety. All eight causes of action arise from Mr. Szabo's allegations that Mr. Rumberger posted false statements about Mr. Szabo on Yelp and other websites and Mr. Rumberger misused the judicial process. (Complaint 3:14-16; 4:2-13; 5:5-12; 5:20-25; 6:17-21; 7:13-16; 9:6-7; 9:20-23.) Mr. Rumberger satisfies his first prong burden of the anti-SLAPP analysis as to all eight causes of action. The posting of consumer information on Yelp implicates protected activity per the anti-SLAPP statute. (Wong v. Tai Jing (2010) 189 Cal.App.4th 1354, 1366-1367.) The allegations that Mr. Rumberger posted information about Mr. Szabo's fraudulent activities and penchant to engage in sexual misconduct raise issues of public interest and the postings were made in public fora. The alleged litigation misconduct also implicates the anti-SLAPP statute and claims for malicious prosecution and abuse of process fall within the scope of protected activity by the anti-SLAPP statute. (Jarrow Formulas, Inc. v. LaMarche (2003) 31 Cal.4th 728, 734-735.) Because Mr. Rumberger satisfied his first prong burden, the burden shifts to Mr. Szabo to present evidence showing at least minimal merit of his claims. Mr. Szabo fails to meet that burden. Mr. Szabo does not present admissible evidence to support any of his claims. Order signed. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR #8189

* * * After the hearing the court vacated its ruling and ruled as follows - DEFENDANT TIMOTHY RUMBERGER'S Special Anti-Slapp Motion To Strike, as to all eight causes of action in the complaint filed by plaintiff Tibor Szabo is continued to July 6, 2017. Any further briefings to be filed by July 3 with courtesy copies to be delivered to Dept. 302 on the same day. The Court invalidated the order that was signed earlier * *
